annotations.label: Discrete Fourier transform

anchor.snippet: the discrete Fourier transform ( DFT ) is a discrete version of the Fourier transform that converts a finite sequence of numbers into another sequence of the same length

annotations.note: `ZMod.dft` defines the DFT on functions `ZMod N → E` as a bundled ℂ-linear equivalence.

annotations.label: Inverse DFT

anchor.snippet: The inverse discrete Fourier transform reverses this process and recovers the original sequence.

annotations.note: Since `ZMod.dft` is a `LinearEquiv`, its inverse is `LinearEquiv.symm ZMod.dft` (notation `𝓕⁻`) with formula in `ZMod.invDFT_apply`.

annotations.label: DFT formula

anchor.snippet: The discrete Fourier transform transforms a sequence of N complex numbers

annotations.note: `ZMod.dft_apply` gives `𝓕 Φ k = ∑ j, stdAddChar (-(j*k)) • Φ j`, matching the standard DFT formula.

annotations.label: Inverse transform formula

anchor.snippet: The inverse transform is given by:

annotations.note: `ZMod.invDFT_apply` gives `𝓕⁻ Ψ k = N⁻¹ • ∑ j, stdAddChar (j*k) • Ψ j`.

anchor.snippet: the sampling interval can be included explicitly as

annotations.note: No version of the DFT with an explicit sampling-interval normalization was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Inverse DFT with sampling interval

anchor.snippet: The corresponding inverse transform then becomes:

annotations.note: No sampling-interval-scaled inverse DFT is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: DFT computation example

anchor.snippet: This example demonstrates how to apply the DFT to a sequence of length

annotations.note: No concrete worked DFT example sequence is computed in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Linearity of DFT

anchor.snippet: The DFT is a linear transform

annotations.note: `ZMod.dft` is bundled as a `LinearEquiv ℂ`, so additivity and ℂ-homogeneity hold by construction (and `ZMod.dft_const_smul` records homogeneity over commuting scalars).

annotations.label: Time and frequency reversal

anchor.snippet: Reversing the time

annotations.note: `ZMod.dft_comp_neg` states `𝓕 (fun j ↦ Φ (-j)) = fun k ↦ 𝓕 Φ (-k)`, the time/frequency reversal identity.

annotations.label: Conjugation in time

anchor.snippet: If

annotations.note: No lemma relating `𝓕 (conj ∘ Φ)` to `conj ∘ 𝓕 Φ ∘ neg` was found for `ZMod.dft`.

annotations.label: Orthogonality of DFT basis

anchor.snippet: form an orthogonal basis over the set of N -dimensional complex vectors

annotations.note: Mathlib has orthogonality of additive characters on finite abelian groups (`AddChar.wInner_cWeight_eq_boole`), which specializes to the DFT basis, but no direct lemma stated for `ZMod.dft`.

annotations.label: Parseval's theorem

anchor.section: The Plancherel theorem and Parseval's theorem
anchor.snippet: Parseval's theorem states

annotations.note: No Parseval-style equality for `ZMod.dft` was found; Mathlib's Parseval theorems are for `AddCircle`/continuous Fourier, not the finite DFT.

annotations.label: Plancherel theorem

anchor.section: The Plancherel theorem and Parseval's theorem
anchor.snippet: The Plancherel theorem is a special case of Parseval's theorem and states

annotations.note: No Plancherel/L² isometry statement is recorded for `ZMod.dft`.

annotations.label: Periodicity of DFT

anchor.snippet: The periodicity can be shown directly from the definition

annotations.note: `ZMod.dft` outputs are functions on `ZMod N`, so N-periodicity holds by typing; no separately stated lemma exists.

annotations.label: Shift theorem

anchor.snippet: corresponds to a circular shift of the output

annotations.note: No additive shift theorem `𝓕 (Φ(· - k₀))` is recorded; `ZMod.dft_comp_unitMul` handles multiplicative shifts only.

annotations.label: Circular convolution theorem

anchor.section: Circular convolution theorem and cross-correlation theorem
anchor.snippet: which gives rise to the interpretation as a circular convolution

annotations.note: No convolution theorem for `ZMod.dft` (circular convolution ↔ pointwise product) was found.

annotations.label: Cross-correlation theorem

anchor.section: Circular convolution theorem and cross-correlation theorem
anchor.snippet: Similarly, the cross-correlation of

annotations.note: No cross-correlation/DFT identity for ZMod was found.

annotations.label: Uniqueness of DFT

anchor.section: Uniqueness of the Discrete Fourier Transform
anchor.snippet: any linear transform that turns convolution into pointwise product is the DFT up to a permutation of coefficients

annotations.note: Uniqueness/characterization theorem for the DFT was not found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Convolution theorem duality

anchor.snippet: It can also be shown that

annotations.note: No dual convolution theorem for `ZMod.dft` is present.

annotations.label: Trigonometric interpolation polynomial

anchor.snippet: satisfies the interpolation property

annotations.note: No DFT-based trigonometric interpolation result was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: DFT matrix

anchor.snippet: the DFT can be expressed as the DFT matrix , a Vandermonde matrix

annotations.note: Mathlib has `Matrix.vandermonde` and `Matrix.circulant`, but no explicit DFT-matrix definition.

annotations.label: Unitary DFT

anchor.snippet: the DFT becomes a unitary transformation , defined by a unitary matrix

annotations.note: No unitary/normalized version of `ZMod.dft` (with the `1/√N` factor making it a `LinearIsometryEquiv`) was found.

annotations.label: Orthonormality of unitary DFT

anchor.snippet: The orthogonality of the DFT is now expressed as an orthonormality condition

annotations.note: No orthonormality statement for a unitary DFT exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: DFT diagonalizes circulant matrices

anchor.snippet: the DFT matrix F diagonalizes any circulant matrix

annotations.note: `Matrix.circulant` is defined but no connection to a DFT diagonalization is established.

annotations.label: Inverse DFT by input reversal

anchor.section: Expressing the inverse DFT in terms of the DFT
anchor.snippet: we can compute the inverse DFT by reversing all but one of the inputs

annotations.note: `ZMod.invDFT_apply'` states `𝓕⁻ Ψ k = N⁻¹ • 𝓕 Ψ (-k)`, expressing the inverse DFT via the forward DFT and negation.

annotations.label: Inverse DFT by conjugation

anchor.section: Expressing the inverse DFT in terms of the DFT
anchor.snippet: one can also conjugate the inputs and outputs

annotations.note: No conjugate-then-forward-then-conjugate identity for `ZMod.dft` is present.

annotations.label: Inverse DFT by swap real/imaginary

anchor.section: Expressing the inverse DFT in terms of the DFT
anchor.snippet: the inverse transform is the same as the forward transform with the real and imaginary parts swapped

annotations.note: No real/imaginary swap formulation of the inverse DFT is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: DFT eigenvalues are fourth roots of unity

anchor.snippet: the eigenvalues of

annotations.note: `ZMod.dft_dft` gives `𝓕² Φ = N • Φ ∘ neg`, from which `𝓕⁴ = N²·Id` follows; the eigenvalue statement itself is not separately formalized.

annotations.label: Matrix polynomial equation for unitary DFT

anchor.snippet: This matrix satisfies the matrix polynomial equation

annotations.note: `ZMod.dft_dft` records `𝓕² = N • (·∘neg)`, which underlies the polynomial relation; no statement on the unitary DFT matrix polynomial exists.

annotations.label: Projection operator eigenvector construction

anchor.snippet: One method to construct DFT eigenvectors to an eigenvalue

annotations.note: Projection-based DFT eigenvector construction is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Gaussian DFT eigenvector

anchor.snippet: the discretized and periodically summed Gaussian function yields an eigenvector of the discrete transform

annotations.note: No periodized-Gaussian eigenvector of the DFT is recorded in Mathlib.

anchor.snippet: For DFT period N = 2 L = 4 K , where K is an integer, the following are eigenvectors of DFT

anchor.snippet: For DFT period N = 4 K - 1, where K is an integer, the following are eigenvectors of DFT

annotations.note: Closed-form DFT eigenvectors for `N=4K-1` are not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Deterministic uncertainty principle

anchor.snippet: There is also a well-known deterministic uncertainty principle that uses signal sparsity

annotations.note: No sparsity-based (Donoho–Stark) uncertainty principle is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: DFT of real signals is even symmetric

anchor.section: DFT of real and purely imaginary signals
anchor.snippet: is even symmetric

annotations.note: `ZMod.dft_even_iff` is about even functions, not the conjugate-symmetry property of DFTs of real inputs; the latter is not formalized.

annotations.label: DFT of purely imaginary signals is odd symmetric

anchor.section: DFT of real and purely imaginary signals
anchor.snippet: is odd symmetric

annotations.note: `ZMod.dft_odd_iff` concerns odd functions, not the conjugate-antisymmetry of DFTs of imaginary inputs; the latter is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Generalized DFT

anchor.snippet: This is sometimes known as a generalized DFT

annotations.note: No shifted/non-linear-phase generalized DFT was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Centered DFT

anchor.snippet: which is called the centered DFT

annotations.note: No centered DFT variant is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: DFT as z-transform on unit circle

anchor.snippet: The discrete Fourier transform can be viewed as a special case of the z-transform

annotations.note: Mathlib has no z-transform, so no DFT-as-z-transform specialization exists.

annotations.label: Multidimensional DFT

anchor.snippet: The multidimensional DFT of a multidimensional array

annotations.note: No multidimensional DFT on a product `∏ ZMod Nᵢ` is defined in Mathlib.

anchor.snippet: The inverse of the multi-dimensional DFT is, analogous to the one-dimensional case, given by

annotations.note: No inverse multidimensional DFT is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Row-column algorithm

anchor.snippet: The multidimensional DFT can be computed by the composition of a sequence of one-dimensional DFTs along each dimension.

annotations.note: No row-column decomposition of a multidimensional DFT is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Conjugate symmetry for real multidim input

anchor.snippet: the DFT outputs have a conjugate symmetry similar to the one-dimensional case above

annotations.note: No conjugate-symmetry statement for multidimensional DFTs of real inputs is present.

annotations.label: Polynomial multiplication via DFT

anchor.snippet: Suppose we wish to compute the polynomial product

annotations.note: No formalization of FFT-based polynomial multiplication was found.

annotations.label: DFT as cyclic-group representation

anchor.snippet: The DFT can be interpreted as a complex-valued representation of the finite cyclic group

annotations.note: `ZMod.dft_eq_fourier` identifies the DFT with the abstract Fourier integral against `toCircle`, exposing the cyclic-character viewpoint, but no explicit representation-theoretic statement is recorded.
